full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It has been a year, a full liturgical year, since we last worshipped together in this space.<|9.72|> <|10.14|> Today, the second Sunday in Advent, marks the anniversary of our last service as a community gathered.<|20.82|> <|22.14|> Not all anniversaries feel like cause for celebration, do they?<|28.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.36|> Already on that Sunday, our attendance was lighter.<|5.36|> <|6.36|> We practiced passing the piece in sign language rather than hugging and shaking hands.<|13.32|> <|14.08|> We knew things were changing, but I don't believe any of us had a clue how quickly or how much they would change.<|24.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.02|> Nor did we have any idea on that Sunday morning that we would still be a community scattered a full year later.<|10.72|> <|11.72|> A full year.<|12.78|> <|13.96|> Another Lent in a year that, as our bishop so aptly described in her Ash Wednesday sermon,<|20.84|> <|20.84|> as a year that has tasted like grass.<|25.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Lent. Things are looking hopeful though, aren't they? We are getting there. When a few of you have asked me, "What's the whole<|18.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> up.<|1.00|> <|1.00|> I have reminded you that we have this gorgeous old building that inspires with its soaring<|10.24|> <|10.24|> architecture and dazzles with its jewel-tone stained glass.<|16.82|> <|16.82|> But it is an old building.<|18.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And while God brought a miraculous birth for centenarians in Genesis, God has yet to miraculously birth air conditioning, air filtration, or even a breath of airflow in this hundred-year-old space.<|18.06|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.76|> So we wait a bit longer. We watch as things keep getting better, making the best decisions we can<|10.72|> <|11.36|> for our community, wearing masks, getting vaccinated when it's our turn, all the while making concrete plans for our<|22.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> our future, knowing that God is with us through it all, just as God was with Abraham and Sarah,<|10.72|> <|11.34|> repeatedly reminding them of all that was in store for them, reminding them of all the<|19.94|> <|19.94|> good that was yet to come.<|21.82|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|2.28|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Be well, my friends.<|1.34|> <|2.42|> I miss you.<|3.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|29.98|><|endoftext|>
